commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cda697025dcac6fbe13e1b7547bb5ac98d8db13e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Quick Settings Text-to-Speech card, per-provider voice persistence, `tts.setVoice`, `tts.preview`, UI controller wiring, tests, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review. Current main lacks the requested TTS Settings UI, and the PR-head defects are visible by comparing the new facade/method/UI code against the speech-core runtime API, method-scope contract, and dynamic voice-list provider contract.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are concrete, file-scoped contract repairs that an automated repair lane can attempt before normal maintainer review.

target.title: feat(ui): add TTS toggle, provider selector, voice picker, and sample playback to Quick Settings
target.body:
TTS has been configurable via CLI/config for a while, but the Control UI had zero first-class surface for it — no toggle, no provider selector, no voice picker. Users had to discover and use `openclaw infer tts enable` and friends instead of a settings switch.

## Summary

- **Problem:** No TTS UI in Settings. Enable/disable, provider, and voice were CLI-only; `tts.status` didn't return saved voice prefs; no gateway method existed to persist a default voice or generate a preview. `tts.setVoice` lacked provider validation; `tts.preview` could silently fall back to a different provider and misclassified validation errors as `UNAVAILABLE`.
- **Why it matters:** TTS is a visible capability. Without a UI toggle it feels broken/hidden; real users were reaching for the CLI to do what should be a single click.
- **What changed:** New TTS card in Quick Settings (toggle + provider selector + voice picker + Play sample button), two new gateway methods (`tts.setVoice`, `tts.preview`), `voiceByProvider` persisted to `tts.json`, pref voice applied automatically during synthesis when no explicit voice override is present. `tts.setVoice` now validates the provider via `canonicalizeSpeechProviderId(provider, cfg)` + `getSpeechProvider`, matching the `tts.setProvider` pattern. `tts.preview` now sets `disableFallback: true` when an explicit provider/voice is given and wraps override resolution in its own `try/catch` returning `INVALID_REQUEST` on validation failures. `QuickSettingsProps` gains a `ttsProvidersLoading` prop so the TTS card shows "Loading providers…" instead of a misleading empty state during initial fetch. Unused `loadTtsStatus`/`loadTtsProviders` imports removed from `app-render.ts`. Gateway test file updated to mock `node:fs/promises`/`readFile` (replacing the ineffective `node:fs`/`readFileSync` mock) and expanded with 8 new tests covering `tts.status voiceByProvider`, `tts.setVoice`, and `tts.preview`.
- **What did NOT change:** Existing CLI commands, provider plugin contracts, `tts.convert` path, audio transcode pipeline, and fallback/persona resolution are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature gap, not a regression.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — new feature. Covered by new tests.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/tts.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: TTS card renders in settings grid; toggle, provider/voice selects, and Play sample button render in correct states; `ttsProvidersLoading` shows loading message instead of empty state; gateway tests cover `tts.status voiceByProvider`, `tts.setVoice` (valid/invalid/clear), and `tts.preview` (INVALID_REQUEST, success, `disableFallback`, UNAVAILABLE).
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: rendering tests are cheapest for UI; gateway handler tests cover the new RPC surface without requiring live TTS providers.
- If no new test is added, why not: Tests were added (8 new TTS card/gateway tests).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Quick Settings now has a **Text-to-Speech** card with: toggle ("Read replies aloud when supported"), provider `<select>`, voice `<select>` (filtered to selected provider's voices), "Play sample" button.
- The TTS card shows "Loading providers…" during initial provider fetch instead of a misleading "No TTS providers configured" empty state.
- `tts.status` response gains `voiceByProvider: Record<string, string>`.
- Default voice saved per-provider persists to `~/.openclaw/settings/tts.json` under `tts.voiceByProvider` and is applied automatically on synthesis when no per-call voice override is set.
- New gateway methods: `tts.setVoice { provider, voice }` and `tts.preview { text?, provider?, voice? }` → `{ audioDataUrl, provider, latencyMs }`.
- `tts.setVoice` rejects unregistered provider ids (returns `INVALID_REQUEST`) rather than silently persisting them.
- `tts.preview` with an explicit provider/voice no longer silently falls back; returned `provider` always matches the user's selection.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Settings tab] → [no TTS surface]
[user] → CLI: openclaw infer tts enable / set-provider / ...

After:
[Settings tab] → [TTS card]
  ├─ toggle → tts.enable / tts.disable
  ├─ provider select → tts.setProvider
  ├─ voice select → tts.setVoice  (new, validated) → persisted to tts.json voiceByProvider
  └─ Play sample → tts.preview   (new, disableFallback when explicit) → base64 audio → new Audio().play()

[synthesis path]
  tts.convert / textToSpeech
    └─ if no explicit voice override: read tts.json voiceByProvider[provider]
         └─ merge as { voice, voiceId } into providerConfig before prepareSpeechSynthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No` — `tts.preview` calls the same `textToSpeech` path already exposed by `tts.convert`; same provider auth, same timeout (15 s), no new network surface.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` — calls existing provider SDKs already reachable via `tts.convert`.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No` — reads/writes only to the existing `tts.json` prefs file.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (CI sandbox)
- Runtime/container: Node 20 (CI), targets Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A for UI rendering tests
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Open Control UI → Settings (Quick mode).
2. Locate the **Text-to-Speech** card.
3. Toggle TTS on → verify `tts.enable` RPC fires.
4. Select a configured provider → `tts.setProvider`.
5. Select a voice from the filtered list → `tts.setVoice`.
6. Click **Play sample** → short audio plays in-browser.

### Expected

- Card renders with correct enabled/disabled state.
- Card shows "Loading providers…" while providers are being fetched; no false "No TTS providers configured" flash.
- Provider/voice selects reflect saved prefs on reload.
- "Play sample" plays audio without page reload; returned provider matches selected provider.
- Saved voice is applied on next TTS synthesis call.

### Actual

- All rendering tests pass (20 total: 12 existing + 8 new TTS card tests).
- All gateway tests pass (9 total: 1 existing + 8 new `tts.setVoice`/`tts.preview`/`tts.status` tests).
- `check:changed` exits 0 (typecheck, lint, import-cycles, all guards).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

`pnpm test config-quick.test.ts tts.test.ts` → 29 tests pass (20 UI + 9 gateway). `pnpm check:changed` → exit 0.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: TypeScript typecheck clean across core, core-tests, extensions, extension-tests lanes; lint clean; import cycle count unchanged (0). `tts.setVoice` rejects unknown provider ids. `tts.preview` passes `disableFallback: true` when provider/voice explicitly given. Override resolution failure returns `INVALID_REQUEST`. Loading state renders correctly.
- Edge cases checked: `providerOverrides` with non-voice keys (model, temperature) no longer suppress pref-voice — only an explicit `voice`/`voiceId` field in the override does. Empty voice select (`value=""`) maps to `null` (clear pref) via explicit `.length > 0` check, not falsy coercion. `ttsProvidersLoading: true` with empty `ttsProviders` shows loading message, not empty state.
- What you did **not** verify: Live provider playback end-to-end (requires configured TTS provider and Node 22 runtime with `node:sqlite`).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — `voiceByProvider` is optional in prefs; existing `tts.json` files without the key are handled gracefully (returns `{}`).
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `tts.preview` reads the synthesized audio file asynchronously using `readFile` from `node:fs/promises` to avoid event loop stall on large audio buffers.
  - Mitigation: async `readFile` from `node:fs/promises`; 15 s timeout on synthesis itself.
- Risk: Pref voice silently ignored when caller passes `providerOverrides` for non-voice purposes (model, format, etc.).
  - Mitigation: Override check is now field-specific (`voice`/`voiceId` presence), not "any overrides object exists".
- Risk: `tts.setVoice` previously accepted and saved any provider id string, including unregistered ones.
  - Mitigation: Provider is now validated via `canonicalizeSpeechProviderId(provider, cfg)` + `getSpeechProvider(provider, cfg)`, returning `INVALID_REQUEST` for unknown providers.
- Risk: `tts.preview` could silently fall back to a different provider when an explicit provider/voice was requested.
  - Mitigation: `disableFallback: true` is set whenever an explicit provider or voice param is present.
target.author: app/copilot-swe-agent
